old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-wf4j","title":"[cross-impl] Trickle reset condition inconsistent (Python != vs Rust/C \u003e)","description":"After commit 5e72e6b03 aligned C (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/trickle.c:114) and Rust (rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:178) reset() to `if stopped or interval \u003e imin`, Python (python/src/lichen/rpl/trickle.py:90) still uses `if _generation==0 or interval != imin`. RFC 6206 rule 6 says reset if I \u003e Imin. This can cause divergent behavior on edge cases (interval \u003c imin, initial state, generation proxy vs explicit state). Matches comments in rpl_trickle.h:156 but not Python reference impl. Test vectors and sim tests may not catch interop timing diffs. See lichen_rpl_dodag and trickle tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T03:09:52.286370178Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T03:09:52.286370178Z"}

new_value: Aligned Python TrickleTimer.reset() condition to > imin per RFC 6206 rule 6, Rust, and C (commit 5e72e6b). Updated docstring. Tests and ruff pass. Matches rpl_trickle.h comment.
